mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that lowers borrowing costs and stimulates equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain exceeding 200,000 jobs signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high payroll periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust supply-side growth, which enhances corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in combined growth scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During demand-driven expansions, rising residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signal strong economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in demand-driven periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) suggests weakening supply-side capacity, which may lead to inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO decline scenarios are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2% signals a tightening monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market, which may lead to wage inflation and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 280) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) creates uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in such environments are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
